description: Paul is held prisoner here for two years. Finally appeals to Caesar.
First port on voyage. Julius the centurion treats Paul kindly.
Ship sails under Cyprus due to headwinds. Shelter from stronger winds.
Ship sails along coast of Asia Minor. Makes port at Myra in Lycia.
Ship has difficulty sailing westward due to strong winds.
Ship puts in at Fair Havens. Paul warns them not to sail further. Winter coming.
Violent storm for 14 days. Ship breaks apart. But all 276 aboard survive as Paul promised.
Paul and all shipwrecked escape to island. Paul is bitten by viper but unharmed. Heals sick.
Ship from Alexandria picks them up. Three-day stop before sailing on.
Ship reaches tip of Italy. They wait for favorable winds.
Roman port. Paul meets believers here who invite him to stay seven days.
Christians from Rome come to meet Paul on the famous Appian Way Road.
Paul arrives in Rome as prisoner. First under house arrest, then awaiting trial before Caesar.
